1: From nasa'; a burden; specifically, tribute, or porterage; figuratively, an utterance, chiefly a doom, especially singing; mental, desire.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
A Heb. prophet. By reduplication from chabaq; embrace; Chabakkuk, the prophet.
A spokesman, speaker, prophet. From naba'; a prophet or inspired man.
See, behold. A primitive root; to gaze at; mentally, to perceive, contemplate; specifically, to have a vision of.
10: To mock, scoff. A primitive root; to disparage, i.e. Ridicule.
To be weighty, judicious or commanding. A primitive root; probably to be heavy, i.e. honorable.
Object of derision. From Shachatsowm; a laughing-stock.
To laugh. A primitive root; to laugh; by implication, to play.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Fortification. Also feminine mibtsarah; from batsar; a fortification, castle, or fortified city; figuratively, a defender.
To heap up. A primitive root; to aggregate.
Dry earth, dust. From aphar; dust; hence, clay, earth, mud.
To capture, seize, take. A primitive root; to catch; generally, to capture or occupy; also to choose; figuratively, to cohere.
11: At that time. A demonstrative adverb; at that time or place; also as a conjunction, therefore.
To pass on or away, pass through. A primitive root; properly, to slide by, i.e. to hasten away, pass on, spring up, pierce or change.
Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.
To pass over, through, or by, pass on. A primitive root; to cross over; used very widely of any transition; specifically, to cover.
This, which, who. For zeh; this or that.
To offend, be guilty. Or mashem; a primitive root; to be guilty; by implication to be punished or perish.
Or kowach; from an unused root meaning to be firm; vigor, literally or figuratively; also a large lizard.
God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.
12: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
The front, of place, time. Or qedmah; from qadam; the front, of place or time; often used adverbially.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Sacred, holy. Or qadosh; from qadash; sacred; God, an angel, a saint, a sanctuary.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To die. A primitive root: to die; causatively, to kill.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
Rock, cliff. Or tsur; from tsuwr; properly, a cliff; generally, a rock or boulder; figuratively, a refuge; also an edge.
A primitive root; to set; intensively, to found; reflexively, to sit down together, i.e. Settle, consult.
To decide, adjudge, prove. A primitive root; to be right; reciprocal, to argue; causatively, to decide, justify or convict.
13: An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To be able, have power. Or yakowl; a primitive root; to be able, literally or morally.
To look. A primitive root; to scan, i.e. Look intently at; by implication, to regard with pleasure, favor or care.
Trouble, labor, toil. From amal; toil, i.e. Wearing effort; hence, worry, wheth. Of body or mind.
To look. A primitive root; to scan, i.e. Look intently at; by implication, to regard with pleasure, favor or care.
To act or deal treacherously. A primitive root; to cover; figuratively, to act covertly; by implication, to pillage.
To cut in, engrave, plow, devise.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
To swallow down, swallow up, engulf. A primitive root; to make away with; generally, to destroy.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
14: To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
A fish. Or dag; from dagah; a fish; or perhaps rather from da'ag; but still better from da'ag; a fish.
A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.
Creeping things, moving things. From ramas; a reptile or any other rapidly moving animal.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To rule, have dominion, reign. A primitive root; to rule.
15: The foe pulls all of them
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
A primitive root; to ascend, intransitively or actively; used in a great variety of senses, primary and secondary, literal and figurative.
A hook, fishhook. Probably from chakah; a hook.
To drag, drag away. A primitive root; to drag off roughly; by implication, to bring up the cud; by analogy, to saw.
A net, a doomed object, extermination. Or cherem; from charam; physical a net; usually a doomed object; abstr. Extermination.
To gather, remove. A primitive root; to gather for any purpose; hence, to receive, take away, i.e. Remove.
A net, fishing net. Or mikmoreth; feminine of makmar; a net.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
Rejoice, be glad. A primitive root; probably to brighten up, i.e. be blithe or gleesome.
To rejoice. Or guwl; a primitive root; properly, to spin round, i.e. Usually rejoice, or fear.
16: Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
To slaughter for sacrifice. A primitive root; to slaughter an animal.
A net, a doomed object, extermination. Or cherem; from charam; physical a net; usually a doomed object; abstr. Extermination.
To make sacrifices smoke. A primitive root; to smoke, i.e. Turn into fragrance by fire.
A net, fishing net. Or mikmoreth; feminine of makmar; a net.
Portion, tract, territory. From chalaq; properly, smoothness; also an allotment.
Greasy, gross, rich. From shaman; greasy, i.e. Gross; figuratively, rich.
Fat. From bara'; fatted or plump.
17: Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
Arm, cast out, draw out, make empty, pour forth out. A primitive root; to pour out, i.e. Empty.
A net, a doomed object, extermination. Or cherem; from charam; physical a net; usually a doomed object; abstr. Extermination.
From an unused root meaning to stretch; properly, continuance; but used only constant; ellipt. The regular sacrifice.
To kill, slay. A primitive root; to smite with deadly intent.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To spare. A primitive root; to commiserate; by implication, to spare.
2: As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
Where? whither?. Or manah; contracted from 'aiyn; where?; hence, whither?, when?; also hither and thither.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To cry out (for help). A primitive root; properly, to be free; but used only causatively and reflexively, to halloo.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
To cry, cry out, call. A primitive root; to shriek; by analogy, to announce or convene publicly.
Violence, wrong. From chamac; violence; by implication, wrong; by meton. Unjust gain.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To deliver. A primitive root; properly, to be open, wide or free, i.e. to be safe; causatively, to free or succor.
3: To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
From an unused root perhaps meaning properly, to pant; strictly nothingness; also trouble. Vanity, wickedness; specifically an idol.
To look. A primitive root; to scan, i.e. Look intently at; by implication, to regard with pleasure, favor or care.
Trouble, labor, toil. From amal; toil, i.e. Wearing effort; hence, worry, wheth. Of body or mind.
Violence, havoc, devastation, ruin. Or showd; from shuwd; violence, ravage.
Violence, wrong. From chamac; violence; by implication, wrong; by meton. Unjust gain.
From nagad; a front, i.e. Part opposite; specifically a counterpart, or mate; usually over against or before.
Strife, dispute. Or rib; from riyb; a contest.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Strife, contention. From diyn; a contest or quarrel.
Or nacah ('abad); a primitive root; to lift, in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, absol. And rel.
4: Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
Direction, instruction, law. Or torah; from yarah; a precept or statute, especially the Decalogue or Pentateuch.
To grow numb. A primitive root; to be sluggish.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
To surround. A primitive root; to enclose; hence to crown, to besiege; also to wait.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
To bend, twist. A primitive root; to wrest.
5: To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
To look. A primitive root; to scan, i.e. Look intently at; by implication, to regard with pleasure, favor or care.
To be astounded or dumbfounded. A primitive root; to be in consternation.
To be astounded or dumbfounded. A primitive root; to be in consternation.
Do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, especially to practise.
Doing, deed, work. From pa'al; an act or work.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
6: Lo! behold! A primitive particle; lo!; also if.
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
A region of S. Bab. and its inhab. (occasionally with enclitic) Kasdiymah; towards the Kasdites.
Bitter, bitterness. Or marah; from marar; bitter; also bitterness, or bitterly.
To hasten. A primitive root; properly, to be liquid or flow easily, i.e. to hurry; often used adverbially, promptly.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
A broad or roomy place. From rachab; enlargement, either literally, or figuratively.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
Or yaresh; a primitive root; to occupy; by implication, to seize, to rob, to inherit; also to expel, to impoverish, to ruin.
Dwelling place, tabernacle. From shakan; a residence; specifically, the Tabernacle.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
7: Terrible, dreadful. From an unused root; frightful.
To fear, to revere, caus, to frighten. A primitive root; to fear; morally, to revere; caus. To frighten.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
From nasa'; an elevation or leprous scab; figuratively, elation or cheerfulness; exaltation in rank or character.
8: A swallow, swift (type of bird). Or cuc; from an unused root meaning to skip; a horse; also a swallow.
To be slight, swift or trifling. A primitive root; to be light, literally Or figuratively.
From an unused root meaning properly, to filtrate, i.e. Be limpid; and thus to spot or stain as if by dripping; a leopard.
To be sharp, keen. A primitive root; to be sharp or severe.
A wolf. From an unused root meaning to be yellow; a wolf.
A steed, a driver, cavalry. From parash; a steed (compare cuwc); also a driver, i.e. cavalry.
Probably to spring about. A primitive root; to spread; figuratively, act proudly.
A steed, a driver, cavalry. From parash; a steed (compare cuwc); also a driver, i.e. cavalry.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Or rachoq; from rachaq; remote, literally or figuratively, of place or time; specifically, precious; often used adverbially.
To fly, to faint. A primitive root; to cover; hence to fly; also to faint.
An eagle. From an unused root meaning to lacerate; the eagle.
A primitive root; to hurry; figuratively, to be eager with excitement or enjoyment.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
9: The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Perhaps assembling. From the same as gam; properly, accumulation, i.e. Impulse or direction.
Violence, wrong. From chamac; violence; by implication, wrong; by meton. Unjust gain.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
East, east wind. Or qadim; from qadam; the fore or front part; hence the East.
To gather, remove. A primitive root; to gather for any purpose; hence, to receive, take away, i.e. Remove.
Exiled, captured, exile, booty. From shbuw; exiled; captured; as noun, exile; by extension, booty.

1: To take one's stand, stand. A primitive root; to stand, in various relations.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Fem. Of mishmar; watch, i.e. The act, or The sentry, the post; obj. Preservation, or Safe; fig. Observance, i.e. Duty or A usage or party.
To set or station oneself, take one's stand. A primitive root; to place; reflexively, to station, offer, continue.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Siege enclosure, siege, entrenchment. Or matsuwr; from tsuwr; something hemming in, i.e. a mound, a siege, distress; or a fastness.
A primitive root; properly, to lean forward, i.e. To peer into the distance; by implication, to observe, await.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
And towkachath; from yakach; chastisement; figuratively correction, refutation, proof.
10: To advise, counsel. A primitive root; to advise; reflexively, to deliberate or resolve.
Shame, shameful thing. From buwsh; shame; by implication an idol.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
To cut off. A primitive root; to cut off; to destroy; to scrape off.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
A primitive root; properly, to miss; hence to sin; by inference, to forfeit, lack, expiate, repent, lead astray, condemn.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
11: A stone. From the root of banah through the meaning to build; a stone.
To cry, cry out, call. A primitive root; to shriek; by analogy, to announce or convene publicly.
A wall. Or qir; or qiyrah; from quwr; a wall.
A rafter, girder. From an unused root meaning to connect; a girder.
Tree, trees, wood. From atsah; a tree; hence, wood.
12: Ah! alas! ha! A prolonged form of how; oh!
To build. A primitive root; to build.
Excitement. Or par; or ayar; from uwr a city in the widest sense.
Blood. From damam; blood of man or an animal; by analogy, the juice of the grape; figuratively bloodshed.
A primitive root; properly, to be erect; hence to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal, or figurative.
A town, city. From qarah in the sense of flooring, i.e. Building; a city.
Injustice, unrighteousness. Or lavel; and lavlah; or owlah; or.olah; from aval; evil.
13: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Lo! behold! Prolongation for hen; lo!
With (denoting proximity). Probably from 'anah; properly, nearness, near; hence, generally, with, by, at, among, etc.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Or tsbadah; from tsaba'; a mass of persons, especially reg. Organized for war; by implication, a campaign, literally or figuratively.
To toil, grow or be weary. A primitive root; properly, to gasp; hence, to be exhausted, to tire, to toil.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
Sufficiency, enough. Of uncertain derivation; enough, used chiefly with preposition in phrases.
A fire. A primitive word; fire.
People. Or l owm; from an unused root meaning to gather; a community.
To be weary, faint. A primitive root; to tire.
Sufficiency, enough. Of uncertain derivation; enough, used chiefly with preposition in phrases.
Emptiness, a worthless thing, in vain. From ruwq; emptiness; figuratively, a worthless thing; adverbially, in vain.
14: Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
To be full, to fill. Or malae; a primitive root, to fill or be full of, in a wide application.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Rarely kabod; from kabad; properly, weight, but only figuratively in a good sense, splendor or copiousness.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
To cover. A primitive root; properly, to plump, i.e. Fill up hollows; by implication, to cover.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.
15: Ah! alas! ha! A prolonged form of how; oh!
Cause to drink water, give to drink. A primitive root; to quaff, i.e. to irrigate or furnish a potion to.
Friend, companion, fellow. Or reya2; from ra'ah; an associate.
To join, attach to. Or saphach; a primitive root; properly, to scrape out, but in certain peculiar senses.
Heat, rage. Or chemac; from yacham; heat; figuratively, anger, poison.
Also, yea. A primitive particle; meaning accession; also or yea; adversatively though.
A primitive root; to become tipsy; in a qualified sense, to satiate with a stimulating drink or influence.
From anah; properly, heed, i.e. Purpose; used only adverbially, on account of, teleologically, in order that.
To look. A primitive root; to scan, i.e. Look intently at; by implication, to regard with pleasure, favor or care.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Nakedness, pudendum. From uwr; nakedness, i.e. the pudenda.
16: To be sated, satisfied or surfeited. Or sabeay; a primitive root; to sate, i.e. Fill to satisfaction.
Ignominy, dishonor. From qalah; disgrace; the pudenda.
Rarely kabod; from kabad; properly, weight, but only figuratively in a good sense, splendor or copiousness.
To drink. A primitive root; to imbibe.
A primitive root; properly, to strip; but used as denominative from arel; to expose or remove the prepuce, whether literal or figurative.
From an unused root meaning to hold together; a cup, often figuratively, a lot; also some unclean bird, probably an owl.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Right hand. From yaman; the right hand or side of a person or other object; locally, the south.
A primitive root; to revolve, surround, or border; used in various applications, literally and figuratively.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Rarely kabod; from kabad; properly, weight, but only figuratively in a good sense, splendor or copiousness.
17: Violence, wrong. From chamac; violence; by implication, wrong; by meton. Unjust gain.
A wooded mountain range on the N. border of Isr. From lbab; white mountain; Lebanon, a mountain range in Palestine.
To cover. A primitive root; properly, to plump, i.e. Fill up hollows; by implication, to cover.
Violence, havoc, devastation, ruin. Or showd; from shuwd; violence, ravage.
A beast, animal, cattle. From an unused root; properly, a dumb beast; especially any large quadruped or animal.
A primitive root; properly, to prostrate; hence, to break down, either by violence, or by confusion and fear.
Blood. From damam; blood of man or an animal; by analogy, the juice of the grape; figuratively bloodshed.
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
Violence, wrong. From chamac; violence; by implication, wrong; by meton. Unjust gain.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
A town, city. From qarah in the sense of flooring, i.e. Building; a city.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.
18: To confer or gain profit or benefit. A primitive root; properly, to ascend; figuratively, to be valuable.
An idol, image. From pacal; an idol.
Probably identical with yatsar;); to mould into a form; especially as a potter; figuratively, to determine (i.e. Form a resolution).
To hew, hew into shape. A primitive root; to carve, whether wood or stone.
From nacak; properly, a pouring over, i.e. Fusion of metal; by implication, a libation, i.e. League; concretely a coverlet.
Or yara; a primitive root; properly, to flow as water; transitively, to lay or throw; figuratively, to point out, to teach.
Deception, disappointment, falsehood. From shaqar; an untruth; by implication, a sham.
Probably identical with yatsar;); to mould into a form; especially as a potter; figuratively, to determine (i.e. Form a resolution).
To trust. A primitive root; properly, to hie for refuge; figuratively, to trust, be confident or sure.
A form, framing, purpose. From yatsar; a form; figuratively, conception.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Insufficiency, worthlessness. Apparently from 'al; good for nothing, by anal. Vain or vanity; specifically an idol.
19: Ah! alas! ha! A prolonged form of how; oh!
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Tree, trees, wood. From atsah; a tree; hence, wood.
Arise, be awake, watch. A primitive root (compare yaqats); to awake.
A silence, in silence, silently. From damam; still; adverbially, silently.
A stone. From the root of banah through the meaning to build; a stone.
To rouse oneself, awake. A primitive root; to wake.
Or yara; a primitive root; properly, to flow as water; transitively, to lay or throw; figuratively, to point out, to teach.
Lo! behold! Prolongation for hen; lo!
A primitive root; to manipulate, i.e. Seize; chiefly to capture, wield, specifically, to overlay; figuratively, to use unwarrantably.
Gold. From an unused root meaning to shimmer; gold, figuratively, something gold-colored, as oil, a clear sky.
Silver, money. From kacaph; silver; by implication, money.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.
Inward part, midst. From qarab; properly, the nearest part, i.e. The center, whether literal, figurative or adverbial.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
2: The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
To write. A primitive root; to grave, by implication, to write.
Vision. From chazah; a sight, i.e. A dream, revelation, or oracle.
To make distinct or plain. A primitive root; to dig; by analogy, to engrave; figuratively, to explain.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A tablet, board or plank, a plate. Or luach; from a primitive root; probably meaning to glisten; a tablet, of stone, wood or metal.
From anah; properly, heed, i.e. Purpose; used only adverbially, on account of, teleologically, in order that.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
To run. A primitive root; to run.
20: The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Apartness, sacredness. From qadash; a sacred place or thing; rarely abstract, sanctity.
A palace, temple. Probably from yakol; a large public building, such as a palace or temple.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
Hush! keep silence! A primitive root; to hush.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
3: Vision. From chazah; a sight, i.e. A dream, revelation, or oracle.
Or rod; from uwd; properly, iteration or continuance; used only adverbially, again, repeatedly, still, more.
To breathe, blow. A primitive root; to puff, i.e. Blow with the breath or air; hence, to fan, to utter, to kindle, to scoff.
End. Contracted from qatsats; an extremity; adverbially after.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To lie, be a liar. A primitive root; to lie, literally or figuratively.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To linger, tarry. Apparently a denominative from mah; properly, to question or hesitate, i.e. to be reluctant.
To wait, await. A primitive root; properly, to adhere to; hence, to await.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To remain behind, tarry. A primitive root; to loiter; by implication to procrastinate.
4: Lo! behold! Prolongation for hen; lo!
To swell, be elated. A primitive root; to swell; figuratively, be elated.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To be smooth, straight, or right. A primitive root; to be straight or even; figuratively, to be right, pleasant, prosperous.
To live, to revive. A primitive root; to live, whether literally or figuratively; causatively, to revive.
); or (shortened) >emunah feminine of 'emuwn; literally firmness; figuratively security; morally fidelity.
5: Wine. From an unused root meaning to effervesce; wine; by implication, intoxication.
Also, yea. A primitive particle; meaning accession; also or yea; adversatively though.
To act or deal treacherously. A primitive root; to cover; figuratively, to act covertly; by implication, to pillage.
Proud, haughty. Probably from the same as har; elated; hence, arrogant.
Man. From gabar; properly, a valiant man or warrior; generally, a person simply.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To rest, to celebrate. A primitive root; to rest; causatively (compare naveh), to celebrate.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To be or grow wide or large. A primitive root; to broaden.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
Or shol; from sha'al; Hades or the world of the dead, including its accessories and inmates.
Death. From muwth; death; concretely, the dead, their place or state; figuratively, pestilence, ruin.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To be sated, satisfied or surfeited. Or sabeay; a primitive root; to sate, i.e. Fill to satisfaction.
To gather, remove. A primitive root; to gather for any purpose; hence, to receive, take away, i.e. Remove.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
To gather, collect. A primitive root; to grasp, i.e. Collect.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
6: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
These. Prolonged from 'el; these or those.
Or nacah ('abad); a primitive root; to lift, in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, absol. And rel.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Satire, a mocking poem. From luwts; an aphorism; also a satire.
A riddle, an enigmatic, perplexing saying or question. From chuwd; a puzzle, hence, a trick, conundrum, sententious maxim.
Ah! alas! ha! A prolonged form of how; oh!
To be or become much, many or great. A primitive root; to increase.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Or kabed; a primitive root; to be heavy, i.e. In a bad sense or in a good sense (in the same two senses).
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Weight of pledges, heavy debts. From abat; something pledged, i.e. pawned goods.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
When?. From an unused root meaning to extend; properly, extent; but used only adverbially, when.
7: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to strike with a sting; figuratively, to oppress with interest on a loan.
From an unused root meaning to open; a wink, i.e. Moment (with or without preposition) adverbially, quickly or unexpectedly).
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
To tremble, quake. A primitive root; properly, to shake off, i.e. to agitate.
To awake. A primitive root; to awake.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
8: To drop, strip, to plunder. A primitive root; to drop or strip; by implication, to plunder.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
From yathar; properly, an overhanging, i.e. an excess, superiority, remainder; also a small rope.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
To drop, strip, to plunder. A primitive root; to drop or strip; by implication, to plunder.
Blood. From damam; blood of man or an animal; by analogy, the juice of the grape; figuratively bloodshed.
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
Violence, wrong. From chamac; violence; by implication, wrong; by meton. Unjust gain.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
A town, city. From qarah in the sense of flooring, i.e. Building; a city.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.
9: Ah! alas! ha! A prolonged form of how; oh!
To cut off, break off, gain by violence. A primitive root to break off, i.e. plunder; figuratively, to finish, or stop.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
Gain made by violence, unjust gain, profit. From batsa'; plunder; by extension, gain.
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
A nest. Contracted from qanan; a nest, sometimes including the nestlings; figuratively, a chamber or dwelling.
Height. From ruwm; altitude, i.e. Concretely, abstractly (elation), or adverbially (aloft).
To strip, plunder, deliver oneself, be delivered, snatch away, deliver.
Hollow or flat of the hand, palm, sole (of the foot), a pan. From kaphaph; the hollow hand or palm; figuratively, power.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.

1: Prayer. From palal; intercession, supplication; by implication, a hymn.
A Heb. prophet. By reduplication from chabaq; embrace; Chabakkuk, the prophet.
A spokesman, speaker, prophet. From naba'; a prophet or inspired man.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Or shiggayonah; from shagah; properly, aberration, i.e. a dithyramb or rambling poem.
10: Mountain, hill, hill country. A shortened form of harar; a mountain or range of hills.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
Or chiyl; a primitive root; properly, to twist or whirl, i.e. to dance, to writhe in pain or fear; figuratively, to wait, to pervert.
A flood of rain, rainstorm, downpour. From zaram; a gush of water.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
To pass over, through, or by, pass on. A primitive root; to cross over; used very widely of any transition; specifically, to cover.
Deep, sea, abyss. Or thom; from huwm; an abyss, especially the deep.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
Or nacah ('abad); a primitive root; to lift, in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, absol. And rel.
On high. From ruwm; elevation, i.e. aloft.
11: From an unused root meaning to be brilliant; the sun; by implication, the east; figuratively, a ray, i.e. A notched battlement.
Moon. From the same as yerach; the moon.
To take one's stand, stand. A primitive root; to stand, in various relations.
Elevation, height, lofty abode. Or zbul; from zabal; a residence.
A light. From 'owr; illumination or luminary.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
From chatsats; properly, a piercer, i.e. An arrow; by implication, a wound; figuratively, thunder-bolt; the shaft of a spear.
Lightning. From baraq; lightning; by analogy, a gleam; concretely, a flashing sword.
A spear. From chanah; a lance.
12: To step, march. A primitive root; to pace, i.e. Step regularly; to mount; to march; to hurl.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
Indignation. From za'am; strictly froth at the mouth, i.e. fury.
To tread, thresh. Or dowsh; or diysh; a primitive root; to trample or thresh.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
13: A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
Deliverance, rescue, salvation, safety, welfare. Or yeshai; from yasha'; liberty, deliverance, prosperity.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
Deliverance, rescue, salvation, safety, welfare. Or yeshai; from yasha'; liberty, deliverance, prosperity.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Anointed. From mashach; anointed; usually a consecrated person; specifically, the Messiah.
A primitive root; to dash asunder; by implication, to crush, smash or violently plunge; figuratively, to subdue or destroy.
Head. From an unused root apparently meaning to shake; the head, whether literal or figurative.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
To be naked or bare. A primitive root; to be bare; hence, to empty, pour out, demolish.
Or tsavvar; or tsavvaron; or tsavva.rah; intensively from tsuwr in the sense of binding; the back of the neck.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
Foundation, base. From yacad; a foundation.
To lift up, exalt. From calah; suspension, i.e. Pause.
14: Or mattah; from natah; a branch; figuratively, a tribe; also a rod, whether for chastising, ruling, throwing, or walking.
To puncture. A primitive root; to puncture, literally or figuratively.
Head. From an unused root apparently meaning to shake; the head, whether literal or figurative.
Perhaps warriors. From an unused root meaning to separate, i.e. Decide; a chieftain.
To storm, rage. A primitive root; to rush upon; by implication, to toss.
To be dispersed or scattered. A primitive root; to dash in pieces, literally or figuratively.
Like, as, when. Or kamow; a form of the prefix k-, but used separately; as, thus, so.
A secret place, hiding place. From cathar; properly, a concealer, i.e. A covert.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
Poor, afflicted, humble. From anah; depressed, in mind or circumstances.
15: To tread, march. A primitive root; to tread; by implication, to walk; also to string a bow.
A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.
A swallow, swift (type of bird). Or cuc; from an unused root meaning to skip; a horse; also a swallow.
From chamar; properly, a bubbling up, i.e. Of water, a wave; of earth, mire or clay; also a heap; hence, a chomer or dry measure.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
16: To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
To be agitated, quiver, quake, be excited, perturbed. A primitive root; to quiver.
From an unused root probably meaning to be hollow; the belly, especially the womb; also the bosom or body of anything.
Or sepheth; probably from caphah or shaphah through the idea of termination; the lip; by implication, language; by analogy, a margin.
To tingle, quiver. A primitive root; to tinkle, i.e. Rattle together.
Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Bone, substance, self. From atsam; a bone; by extension, the body; figuratively, the substance, i.e. Selfsame.
To be agitated, quiver, quake, be excited, perturbed. A primitive root; to quiver.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
Underneath, below, instead of. From the same as Towach; the bottom; only adverbially, below, in lieu of, etc.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Tightness, a female rival. Feminine of tsar; tightness; transitively, a female rival.
A primitive root; to ascend, intransitively or actively; used in a great variety of senses, primary and secondary, literal and figurative.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
To invade, attack. A primitive root; to crowd upon, i.e. Attack.
17: Fig tree. Or t:enah; perhaps of foreign derivation; the fig.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to break forth as a bud, i.e. Bloom; generally, to spread; specifically, to fly; figuratively, to flourish.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
Produce (of the soil). From yabal; produce, i.e. A crop or wealth.
A vine. From an unused root meaning to bend; a vine, especially the grape.
Olive tree, olive. Probably from an unused root; an olive, the tree, the branch or the berry.
A deed, work. From asah; an action; generally, a transaction; abstractly, activity; by implication, a product or property.
To disappoint, deceive, fail, grow lean. A primitive root; to be untrue, in word or deed.
A field. Apparently from sadeh; a cultivated field.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Small cattle, sheep and goats, flock. Or tsaown; from an unused root meaning to migrate; a collective name for a flock; also figuratively.
To cut, divide. A primitive root; to cut down or off; to destroy, divide, exclude, or decide.
An enclosure, fold. Or miklah; from kala'; a pen.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
Cattle, herd, an ox. From baqar; beef cattle or an animal of the ox family of either gender; collectively, a herd.
Stable, stall. Probably from raphah; a stall for cattle.
18: To exult. A primitive root; to jump for joy, i.e. Exult.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To rejoice. Or guwl; a primitive root; properly, to spin round, i.e. Usually rejoice, or fear.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Deliverance, rescue, salvation, safety, welfare. Or yeshai; from yasha'; liberty, deliverance, prosperity.
19: Lord. Am emphatic form of 'adown; the Lord.
From chuwl; probably a force, whether of men, means or other resources; an army, wealth, virtue, valor, strength.
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
Foot. From ragal; a foot; by implication, a step; by euphem. The pudenda.
A hind, doe. Feminine of 'ayal; a doe or female deer.
To tread, march. A primitive root; to tread; by implication, to walk; also to string a bow.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A high place. From an unused root; an elevation.
Preeminent or enduring. A primitive root; properly, to glitter from afar, i.e. To be eminent; also, to be permanent.
2: The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
A hearing, report. From shama'; something heard, i.e. A sound, rumor, announcement; abstractly, audience.
To fear, to revere, caus, to frighten. A primitive root; to fear; morally, to revere; caus. To frighten.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Doing, deed, work. From pa'al; an act or work.
To live, to revive. A primitive root; to live, whether literally or figuratively; causatively, to revive.
Inward part, midst. From qarab; properly, the nearest part, i.e. The center, whether literal, figurative or adverbial.
A year. (in plura or shanah; from shanah; a year.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Inward part, midst. From qarab; properly, the nearest part, i.e. The center, whether literal, figurative or adverbial.
A year. (in plura or shanah; from shanah; a year.
Agitation, excitement, raging. From ragaz; commotion, restlessness, crash, disquiet, anger.
Remember. A primitive root; properly, to mark, i.e. To remember; by implication, to mention; also to be male.
To love, have compassion. A primitive root; to fondle; by implication, to love, especially to compassionate.
3: God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Or Teman; the same as teyman; Teman, the name of two Edomites, and of the region and descendant of one of them.
Sacred, holy. Or qadosh; from qadash; sacred; God, an angel, a saint, a sanctuary.
Mountain, hill, hill country. A shortened form of harar; a mountain or range of hills.
A place in Sinai. From pa'ar; ornamental; Paran, a desert of Arabia.
To lift up, exalt. From calah; suspension, i.e. Pause.
Splendor, majesty, vigor. From an unused root; grandeur.
To cover. A primitive root; properly, to plump, i.e. Fill up hollows; by implication, to cover.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
Praise, song of praise. From halal; laudation; specifically a hymn.
To be full, to fill. Or malae; a primitive root, to fill or be full of, in a wide application.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
4: To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
A light. From 'owr; illumination or luminary.
From qaran; a horn; by implication, a flask, cornet; by resembl. An elephant's tooth, a corner, a peak, a ray; figuratively, power.
There, thither. A primitive particle; there then; often thither, or thence.
Strength, might. Or rowz; from azaz; strength in various applications.
A hiding, hiding place. From chabah; a concealment.
5: To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
Flame. From saraph; a live coal; by analogy lightning; figuratively, an arrow,; specifically, fever.
A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
Foot. From ragal; a foot; by implication, a step; by euphem. The pudenda.
6: To take one's stand, stand. A primitive root; to stand, in various relations.
To shake. A primitive root; to shake.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
A primitive root; to jump, i.e. Be violently agitated; causatively, to terrify, shake off, untie.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
Perpetuity. From adah; properly, a terminus, i.e. duration, in the sense of advance or perpetuity.
Hill, mountain. From an unused root meaning to loom up; a mountain.
To be dispersed or scattered. A primitive root; to dash in pieces, literally or figuratively.
A hill. Feminine from the same as Geba'; a hillock.
To bow, be bowed down, crouch. A primitive root; to sink or depress.
A going, way, traveling company. Feminine of haliyk; a walking; by implication, a procession or march, a caravan.
7: To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
A region of Arabia. Perhaps from Kuwsh; Cushan, a region of Arabia.
Underneath, below, instead of. From the same as Towach; the bottom; only adverbially, below, in lieu of, etc.
From an unused root perhaps meaning properly, to pant; strictly nothingness; also trouble. Vanity, wickedness; specifically an idol.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
The same as midyan; Midjan, a son of Abraham; also his country and his descendants.
To be agitated, quiver, quake, be excited, perturbed. A primitive root; to quiver.
8: To burn or be kindled with anger. A primitive root; to glow or grow warm; figuratively to blaze up, of anger, zeal, jealousy.
A stream, river. From nahar; a stream; figuratively, prosperity.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
A stream, river. From nahar; a stream; figuratively, prosperity.
Overflow, arrogance, fury. Feminine of eber; an outburst of passion.
A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.
To mount and ride, ride. A primitive root; to ride; causatively, to place upon, to despatch.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A swallow, swift (type of bird). Or cuc; from an unused root meaning to skip; a horse; also a swallow.
A chariot. Feminine of merkab; a chariot.
Salvation. Feminine passive participle of yasha'; something saved, i.e. deliverance; hence, aid, victory, prosperity.
9: To be exposed or bare. A primitive root; to bare.
A bow. From qashah in the original sense of bending: a bow, for shooting or the iris.
Promise, speech, thing, word. The same as 'emer.
An oath, curse. Feminine passive participle of shaba'; properly, something sworn, i.e. An oath.
Or mattah; from natah; a branch; figuratively, a tribe; also a rod, whether for chastising, ruling, throwing, or walking.
To lift up, exalt. From calah; suspension, i.e. Pause.
To cleave, break open or through. A primitive root; to cleave; generally, to rend, break, rip or open.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
A stream, river. From nahar; a stream; figuratively, prosperity.
